name: Orange Custard Cookies
recipeIngredient: 2 teaspoons Orange flower water or 1 teaspoon Vanilla extract
recipeInstructions:
DESCRIPTION: Tiny cookie tartlet crusts with an orange-almond filling.
CRUST: In a food processor, combine flour, sugar, and salt. Add cold butter cubes (about 3/4 cup) and process until the mixture resembles coarse meal. Gradually add egg, blending just until mixed. If needed, add drops of chilled water until the dough holds together. Knead the dough for about 15 seconds to form a ball. Cover and refrigerate for 15 minutes.
FILLING: In a bowl, combine egg, whipping cream, and sugar. Add remaining ingredients (almonds, orange zest, half an orange, orange flower water or vanilla extract, nutmeg, and salt) and mix well until combined.
Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Lightly butter 24 miniature muffin tins.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the dough to about 1/8 inch thick. Using a 2-5/2-inch scalloped cutter, cut out rounds and place one in each muffin tin, pressing firmly over the bottom and sides. Fill each shell about half full with custard mixture.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and a knife inserted into the center comes out clean. Using the tip of a pointed knife, loosen the cookies from the tins and let cool on wire racks. Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 5 days. Does not freeze well.
